title: Hellmann Calipar Healthcare Logistics expands regional operations with new Dubai South facility
summary: Hellmann's new Dubai South location responds directly to these mounting pressures by offering comprehensive capabilities designed to meet the sector's most demanding standards.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
UAE—Hellmann Worldwide Logistics has opened a cutting-edge distribution facility in Dubai South’s Logistics District, marking a significant milestone in the company’s regional expansion.

This new centre brings Hellmann’s presence to five dedicated healthcare distribution locations across the United Arab Emirates, solidifying its position as a leading provider of specialized healthcare logistics solutions in the Middle East.

The facility addresses a critical gap in the region’s supply chain infrastructure.

As healthcare markets grow increasingly complex, companies require more sophisticated solutions to manage temperature-sensitive medical products and navigate stringent regulatory requirements.

Hellmann’s new Dubai South location responds directly to these mounting pressures by offering comprehensive capabilities designed to meet the sector’s most demanding standards.

Comprehensive healthcare solutions

The distribution centre features advanced infrastructure tailored specifically for healthcare logistics.

The facility manages time and temperature-sensitive products with precision, ensuring compliance with strict industry regulations.

Additionally, it handles hazardous goods securely while maintaining end-to-end digital transparency throughout the supply chain.

These features enable customers to track shipments in real time and verify that products remain within required parameters at every stage of transit.

Beyond storage, the facility expands Hellmann’s capacity to deliver customer-specific value-added services.

Efficient last-mile distribution capabilities ensure that medical products reach healthcare providers, hospitals, and pharmacies reliably and on schedule.

The expanded warehouse space allows the company to accommodate growing customer demands while maintaining the precision that healthcare logistics demands.

Supporting regional hub growth

Mohsen Ahmad, CEO of Dubai South’s Logistics District, emphasized the strategic importance of this development.

The expansion reflects the region’s growing reliance on specialized logistics infrastructure as healthcare companies strengthen their presence across the Middle East.

The Logistics District’s integrated infrastructure, strategic geographic positioning, and multimodal connectivity provide the foundation that international companies need to optimize their regional operations and deliver essential products efficiently.

Lee I’Ons, Regional CEO for the Indian, Middle East, and Africa (IMEA) region at Hellmann Worldwide Logistics, highlighted the company’s long-term commitment to the market.

With the fifth healthcare facility now operational in the UAE, Hellmann enhances its responsiveness to customer needs while improving its ability to scale operations quickly.

The expansion reinforces the company’s capacity to provide regulatory assurance, a critical factor as healthcare supply chains face increasingly complex compliance requirements across borders.
